truth_check_history.notes: M1-M7 + L1-L4 all pass. G1 infra_error only — GITHUB_TOKEN absent in local shell env. Waiver PE3 documented in preflight token.

findings.G2_C3: manifest.commit_sha stores short SHA 'e21b6999'; GitHub API returned full 40-char SHA 'e21b6999151268471291e6cdd3608a9568e1fae2'. Strict string comparison fails. Merge is correct — short SHA is a prefix of the full SHA. Harness stores short SHAs; future manifests should store full SHAs.
findings.P10_R3: evidence.json lacks verifier.identity field. PM was the verifier (stateHistory: In PM Review → Done). Evidence schema did not include this field. Implementation correctness unaffected.
findings.R1_R2: evidence.json runtime_proof uses simplified format {pnpm_test_db: 'pass', supabase_project: '...'} instead of {queries: [...], row_counts: [...]} arrays. pnpm test:db DID pass at merge time per Run 1 evidence. Schema mismatch only — no missing runtime test.
findings.post_close_trigger: Runs 2 and 3 were heartbeat-triggered after lane close (heartbeat_at matches Run 3 timestamp exactly). By that time GITHUB_TOKEN was available in environment, enabling GitHub PR fetch and exposing short vs full SHA mismatch. Runs are stale post-close artifacts, not re-validations of the implementation.

pm_waivers.pm_disposition: WAIVED — Run 1 (14:50:20) is the authoritative truth-check. All six failures in Runs 2+3 are post-close harness artifacts: short SHA format, evidence schema gap, post-close heartbeat trigger. Implementation, tests, pnpm test:db, and CI sentinels all passed. Lane remains Done.
